title: hieracium racemosum subsp. insulare
intro: Hieracium racemosum subsp. insulare is a rare, flowering plant species in the dandelion family, known for its yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium racemosum subsp. insulare include its potential in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
